column32: Stock Market Trading and Investment Quotes
Quotation
Buy Right, Sit Tight.
Don’t follow tips, follow knowledge.
In bull markets, everyone is a genius.
column33: Widely used in Bhartiya (Indian) trading circles

column32: Investing is not about timing the market, but about time in the market.
The stock market is a place where money moves from the active to the patient.
If you don’t build your dream, someone else will hire you to help them build theirs.
Think big, think fast, think ahead. Ideas are no one’s monopoly.
The biggest risk is not taking any risk.
Value investing is about buying something for less than what it is worth.
Patience is the key to making big money in the market.
Good investors are those who focus on risk first.
You should always invest in businesses you understand.
Invest for the long term. There is no short-term trading formula for success.
In the stock market, there is no substitute for experience.

column30: Prakash Gurpurb Sri Guru Nanak Dev
column32: Markets are like women—always commanding, mysterious, unpredictable, and volatile.
Risk First, Profit Second
Discipline Beats Intelligence
Patience creates Wealth
Psychology > Strategy
Consistency wins long term
Trend is your friend.
Don’t fight the tape.
Cash combined with courage in a crisis is PRICELESS
There is no single right way to trade the markets.
Win or lose, everybody gets what they want out of the market.
If you can’t take a small loss, sooner or later you will take the mother of all losses.
The big money is not in the buying and selling, but in the waiting.
Waiting helps you as an investor and a lot of people just can’t stand to wait.
Successful investing is about managing risk, not avoiding it.
An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.

column30: Prakash Gurpurb Sri Guru Nanak Dev
column32: Know what you own, and know why you own it.
he essence of investment management is the management of risks, not the management of returns.
Rule No.1: Never lose money. Rule No.2: Never forget Rule No.1.
It’s not the return on your money that matters, it’s the return of your money.
Wide diversification is only required when investors do not understand what they are doing.
Amateurs think about how much money they can make. Professionals think about how much money they could lose.
You don’t need to be right all the time. You just need to lose less when you’re wrong.
The goal of a successful trader is to make the best trades. Money is secondary.
Markets can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent.
The stock market is filled with individuals who know the price of everything, but the value of nothing.
The market doesn’t care what you think.
Investing should be more like watching paint dry or watching grass grow.
Time in the market beats timing the market.
99% of good investing is doing nothing.
The majority of trading errors occur due to a lack of patience.
One day you will thank yourself for learning how to trade.
You can't control the volatility of the markets; you can control the volatility of your actions.
Letting losses run is the most serious mistake made by most investors.
Master one trade setup first, perfect it, own it... only then can you add another setup.
Becoming a successful trader doesn't happen overnight. It takes study, time, and practice.
When you learn to let go of the need to be right, being wrong gradually loses its power to disturb you.
It's going to happen because I'm going to make it happen.
I'll keep reducing my trading size as long as I'm losing.
10 years from now you'll look back and say I'm glad I started trading.
As long as a stock is acting right, and the market is right, do not be in a hurry to take profits.
If you can’t take a small loss, sooner or later you will take the mother of all losses.
We're in the business of making mistakes. Winners make small mistakes, losers make big mistakes.
My biggest losses have always followed my largest profits.
Don't focus on making money, focus on protecting what you have.
Price action analysis reveals the footprint of smart money. By following it, we gain an edge.
Professional traders understand the power of risk reward and how to apply it to every trade they take.
Be at one with the charts. Practice your craft, master your craft.
Trade what you see, not what you think.
Pigs get fat, hogs get slaughtered. In trading like in life, never be afraid to leave the party early.
Traders should stop thinking about how much they can profit and start focusing on how much they are risking.
Having trading discipline is the beginning, keeping discipline is the progress, staying disciplined is the success.
The single most important advice I can give is: learn from your mistakes.
In order to succeed, you first have to be willing to experience failure.
Behind every successful trader there is a lot of unsuccessful years.
Don't make friends with trends, make friends with each candlestick.
It's not the bull side or the bear side, but the right side.
Believe in analysis not forecasting.

column30: Dr. Baba Saheb Ambedkar Jayanti/ Mahavir Jayanti
column32: Stocks are bought not in fear but in hope; they are typically sold out of fear.
What you learn today can improve all your tomorrows.
